column5: Zak Waters was this guy who released all this incredible music in 2014, then started his own little spin-off band from his solo tour, and then never got popular and it makes no sense to me because it's literal perfect brain dead summer music

Seeing Bruce on the 27th, so y'all are gonna get a primer starting with Badlands. This is as rockin as The Boss gets in my opinion. There's so many gems in here: "Poor man wanna be rich, rich man wanna be king, and a king ain't satisfied till he rules everything" sound familiar?? But there's always hope with Bruce! "I believe in the hope and I pray that some day it may raise me above these BADLANDS". Crank it up, kids! "It ain't no sin to be glad you're alive".

column5: A slow one from Bruce. Not many, if any, songs paint such a picture in my head as My Hometown. Heartbreaking but also nostalgic. Heartbreaking because it's nostalgic? I can't say, but it hits the feels in a way that only Bruce can.

Just learn the first verse and chorus of this song and you've got yourself verifiable badass rock'n'roll poetry. Special place in my heart for being the song we'd sing coming back into camp at Running Works in 2006.
DJ Joc forgot to dedicate her song to me last month, I won't make the same mistake ;). Just good old fashioned rockin dedicated to my one and only!
